qa_pairs.question: What is my username and password for the national HPC systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Your password for logging into all new national systems is the same one you use for the CCDB. Your username will be displayed at the top of the page once you've logged in.

qa_pairs.question: Could you describe the different types of HPC systems available?
qa_pairs.answer: The available systems include Arbutus, which is a cloud site allowing users to launch and customize virtual machines. Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual are general-purpose clusters (or supercomputers) with various nodes, including large memory and GPU accelerators. Trillium is a homogeneous cluster designed for large parallel jobs requiring over 1000 cores.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I need help choosing the right HPC system for my needs?
qa_pairs.answer: If the system descriptions are insufficient, you should contact technical support. They will ask specific questions about your software, required resources (such as memory, time, computing power), and job frequency to help you identify the most appropriate system.
